# `breeze lock release-all` clears every lock (file and resource kinds alike)
# held by the calling identity in one call, instead of releasing each lock ID
# by hand — and never touches another identity's locks.
env BREEZE_DIR=$WORK/state

exec breeze identity register admin
cp stdout admin.token
exec breeze role assign deployer admin --as admin --token-file admin.token
exec breeze apply -f pipeline.hcl --as admin --token-file admin.token

exec breeze lock acquire a.txt --as admin --ttl 5m
exec breeze lock acquire b.txt --as admin --ttl 5m
exec breeze deploy claim release deploy --env staging --as admin --token-file admin.token
exec breeze lock acquire c.txt --as bob --ttl 5m

exec breeze lock list --all --json
stdout '"holder":\s*"admin"'
stdout '"holder":\s*"bob"'

exec breeze lock release-all --as admin
stdout 'released.*a\.txt'
stdout 'released.*b\.txt'
stdout 'released.*deploy/deploy/staging'
! stdout 'c\.txt'

# admin's locks are gone; bob's own lock survives untouched.
exec breeze lock list --all --json
! stdout '"holder":\s*"admin"'
stdout '"holder":\s*"bob"'

# Releasing again with nothing held is a friendly no-op, not an error.
exec breeze lock release-all --as admin
stdout 'no locks held'

exec breeze stop

-- pipeline.hcl --
pipeline "release" {
  environments = ["staging"]

  stage "deploy" {
    type          = "deploy"
    fans_out      = true
    required_role = "deployer"
    timeout       = "10s"
    command       = ["/bin/true"]
  }
}
